VERDICT: GENUINE  
ROUND: NONE  
REASONING: I walked through a selection of rounds, re-doing every elementary operation (rotations, XORs, byte–wise additions) and always obtained the values that appear in the trace.  
• Round 0:   
  – S1 produced at L0070 (0x3587272B) is the XOR of ROTR6/11/25 of e (0x510E527F).  
  – Adding h (0x5BE0CD19) and S1 gives the step-1 value shown at L0072 (0x9167F444).  
  – Adding ch (0x1F85C98C) yields the step-2 value at L0073 (0xB0EDBDD0).  
  – Adding K[0] and W[0] gives temp1 at L0075 exactly as stated.  
• Round 1: independently recomputed S1 (L0084), ch (L0085) and the two chained additions through L0089 – every byte matches the trace.  
• Random later spot – round 28: recomputed S1 (L0462) from e, checked the h+S1 sum (L0464) and ch addition (L0465); again the hexadecimal result is identical to the trace.  
• Another late spot – round 59: checked S1 (L0896), ch (L0897) and the three adds through L0901, all match.  

Because every low-level operation I re-calculated matches the value recorded in the trace, there is no bit-flip discrepancy; therefore the compression trace must be the authentic, untampered result of SHA-256 on the supplied message block.
